response.justification: Bogdan presents a credible staff-level trajectory with genuine AI-agent infrastructure exposure at re:cinq — including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — which directly satisfies the AI-native baseline the role treats as table stakes. His TypeScript depth is solid and his polyglot range is a real asset. However, Python is a stated must-have and is entirely absent from both the skills section and every role description, which is a material disqualifier unless undisclosed. The high-throughput distributed systems work is real but concentrated in 2018–2020 at Sociomantic; more recent roles skew toward product feature delivery. Operational ownership and incident-response capability are not described anywhere. A screening call to probe Python fluency and on-call experience would determine whether these are genuine gaps or resume omissions.
strengths: Active AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) directly satisfies the AI-native engineering baseline
Demonstrated high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and fiber-based concurrency
Consistent staff-level scope across a 15-year polyglot career with evidence of technical community leadership (workshops, open-source maintainership, conference talks)
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume — not listed in skills and not mentioned in any role — despite being an explicit must-have technical requirement
High-throughput distributed systems experience is largely confined to 2018–2020; post-2020 roles lean toward product feature engineering rather than large-scale reliability or infrastructure work
No mention of operational ownership, on-call rotations, or incident response, which are listed non-technical requirements for this role
